title: Fermented Dense Egyptian Flour Dumplings
description: A hearty, egg‑free dumpling casserole that marries a sourdough‑infused whole‑wheat dough with a spiced tomato sauce, echoing the rustic flavors of Egypt.
instructions: Combine flour and salt in a large bowl. Add the sourdough starter and water, then knead until a smooth, elastic dough forms.
Cover the dough and let it rest at room temperature for 1 hour so the natural leavening develops.
After resting, shape the dough into 2‑cm spheres, stacking them on parchment paper to prevent sticking.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Gently drop the dumplings and cook for 6–8 minutes, or until they rise and are firm yet chewy.
Drain the dumplings and set aside. In the same pot, heat a little oil and sauté onion and garlic until translucent.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, coriander, cayenne, sugar, and lemon juice. Cook, stirring, for 3 minutes until fragrant.
Return the cooked dumplings to the sauce, mixing gently. Simmer for an additional 2 minutes to let the flavors meld.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon if desired.
